facts: Constellium operates a leased facility in White, Georgia, under its Automotive Structures & Industry (AS&I) segment.
The company's U.S. manufacturing footprint includes owned facilities in Ravenswood, West Virginia; Muscle Shoals, Alabama; and Bowling Green, Kentucky.
Constellium's AS&I segment operates joint ventures in Lakeshore, Ontario, Canada, and Changchun, Jilin Province, China.
The company's business units are Aerospace & Transportation (A&T), Packaging & Automotive Rolled Products (P&ARP), and Automotive Structures & Industry (AS&I).
Constellium's automotive product lines include automotive rolled products, specialty and other thin-rolled products, automotive extruded products, and other extruded products.
In 2025, P&ARP accounted for 60% of total revenue ($5,078 million) and 73% of shipments (1,086 kt), while AS&I accounted for 19% of revenue ($1,579 million) and 13% of shipments (202 kt).
Constellium's total shipments reached 1,495 kt in 2025.
The average all-in aluminum price in the U.S. was $3,930 per ton in 2025, up 38% from $2,851 in 2024.
Constellium's total revenue for 2025 was $8,449 million, with net income of $275 million.

Key executives include CEO Ingrid Joerg (age 56), CFO Jack Guo (age 47), President of A&T Philippe Hoffmann (age 60), President of P&ARP Matthew Perkins (age 58), and President of AS&I Stephane Corre (age 53).
Constellium's restructuring costs were $3 million in 2025, down from $11 million in 2024.
The company's metal price lag component of Adjusted EBITDA was $126 million in 2025 versus $48 million in 2024.
Constellium's U.S. Midwest premium averaged $1,298 per ton in 2025, up 200% from $432 in 2024.
The company tracks capital expenditures and depreciation by segment (A&T, P&ARP, AS&I, H&C) for annual reporting.
Constellium's property, plant, and equipment is tracked by segment and category (Land/Property Rights, Buildings, Machinery/Equipment, Construction Work in Progress, Other).
The company's revenue is geographically diversified across the United States, Germany, France, Spain, United Kingdom, Poland, Czech Republic, and other regions.
Constellium's AS&I segment includes leased facilities in Van Buren, Michigan, and White, Georgia, alongside owned/leased sites in Slovakia and Spain.
The company's Adjusted EBITDA for AS&I was $72 million in 2025, compared to $74 million in 2024.
Constellium's P&ARP segment Adjusted EBITDA was $353 million in 2025, up from $242 million in 2024.
The company's A&T segment Adjusted EBITDA was $339 million in 2025, up from $292 million in 2024.
Constellium's operating cash flows were $489 million in 2025 versus $301 million in 2024.
The company's net decrease in cash and cash equivalents (excluding exchange rate effects) was $35 million in 2025 compared to $73 million in 2024.
Constellium's share repurchase program had approximately $106,121,002 remaining as of December 31, 2025.
The company purchased 2,377,077 shares between October and December 2025 at average prices ranging from $16.01 to $17.65 per share.
Constellium's performance relative to the Russell 2000 Index was 135 versus 134 (base 100 in 2020) for the year ended December 31, 2025.
The company's performance relative to the S&P SmallCap 600 Materials Index was 135 versus 154 for the same period.
Constellium's cost of sales (excluding depreciation and amortization) was $7,262 million in 2025, representing 86% of revenue.
The company's research and development expenses were $51 million in 2025, compared to $49 million in 2024.
Constellium's selling and administrative expenses were $332 million in 2025, up from $313 million in 2024.
The company's finance costs net were $109 million in 2025, compared to $111 million in 2024.
Constellium's income tax expense was $133 million in 2025, up from $75 million in 2024.
The company's unrealized gains on commodities derivatives net were $28 million in 2025 versus $19 million in 2024.
Constellium's realized gains on commodities derivatives net were $8 million in 2025 versus $22 million in 2024.
The company's impairment of assets was $21 million in 2025 compared to $24 million in 2024.
Constellium's share-based compensation expense was $19 million in 2025 versus $25 million in 2024.
The company's pension and other post-employment benefits non-operating gains were $(14) million in 2025 versus $(11) million in 2024.
Constellium's net commercial transaction exposure was -$38 million, with derivatives on commercial transactions contributing -$43 million.
The company's cash in bank and intercompany loans contributed a $99 million positive effect on net income, while borrowings had a -$131 million effect.
A 10% strengthening of the U.S. dollar versus the euro would result in a -$15 million effect on net income and a -$45 million effect on equity for Constellium.

Constellium's tax losses carried forward include indefinite carryforwards in the United States and France, and time-limited carryforwards in Mexico (expires 2027), Germany (indefinite), and Switzerland (expires 2028).
The company's unrecognized tax benefits are tracked by jurisdiction including France, United States, Czech Republic, Switzerland, Canada, China, Germany, and others.
Constellium's current tax expense is split between domestic (France) and foreign jurisdictions annually.
The company's deferred tax expense/benefit is also tracked by domestic (France) and foreign components.

Constellium's income before tax is reconciled from statutory rates in France, United States, Germany, Switzerland, Slovakia, and other jurisdictions, with adjustments for base erosion taxes, valuation allowances, and state/local taxes.
The company's net cash flows used in investing activities were $(309) million in 2025 versus $(313) million in 2024.
Constellium's net cash flows used in financing activities were $(215) million in 2025 versus $(61) million in 2024.

The company's segment revenue for A&T, P&ARP, AS&I, and H&C is broken down by product type including aerospace rolled products, transportation/industry/defense rolled products, packaging rolled products, automotive rolled products, specialty thin-rolled products, automotive extruded products, other extruded products, and other.
Constellium's cost of metal, production costs, and other segment expenses are tracked for each business unit across 2023-2025.
The company's depreciation expense related to property, plant, and equipment is tracked annually for 2023-2025.
The company's unrealized gains/losses on derivatives at fair value through profit and loss net was $56 million in 2025 versus $(1) million in 2024.
Constellium's gains/losses on disposal were $4 million in both 2025 and 2024.
The company's other non-operating income/expenses totaled $(1) million in 2025 versus $(2) million in 2024.
Constellium's interest expense on borrowings, finance leases, and pension benefits are tracked annually for 2023-2025.
The company's net loss on settlement of debt is tracked for 2023-2025.
Constellium's realized and unrealized losses/gains on debt derivatives at fair value are tracked for 2023-2025.
The company's realized and unrealized exchange gains/losses on financing activities net are tracked for 2023-2025.
Constellium's other finance expenses are tracked annually for 2023-2025.
The company's capitalized borrowing costs are tracked for 2023-2025.
Constellium's total finance expenses and net finance costs are reconciled annually for 2023-2025.
The company's domestic (France) and foreign income before tax is broken down annually for 2023-2025.
Constellium's income tax expense calculated at the statutory tax rate is reconciled for France, United States, Germany, Switzerland, Slovakia, and other jurisdictions for 2023-2025.
The company's valuation allowances for deferred income tax assets are tracked annually for 2023-2025.
Constellium's net operating loss carryforwards in the United States have an indefinite period, while other jurisdictions have specific expiration dates or periods.
The company's total comprehensive income is attributed to equity holders of Constellium and non-controlling interests for 2023-2025.

Constellium's ordinary shares outstanding, treasury shares, accumulated other comprehensive income, additional paid-in capital, and retained earnings are tracked through the statement of changes in equity for 2023-2025.
The company's basic and diluted weighted-average ordinary shares outstanding include dilutive effects from non-vested restricted stock units and performance-based restricted stock units.
Constellium's trade receivables gross, allowance for credit losses, and total trade receivables net are reported for 2024 and 2025.
The company's inventories are categorized into finished goods, work in progress, raw materials, and stores and supplies for 2024 and 2025.
Constellium's property, plant, and equipment net balance is reported by category for December 31, 2024 and 2025.
The company's operating lease right-of-use assets and finance lease assets are classified under property, plant, and equipment on the consolidated balance sheets as of December 31, 2024 and 2025.
Constellium's current operating lease liabilities are included in trade payables and other, while non-current operating lease liabilities are reported separately.
The company's current finance lease liabilities are included in short-term debt, with non-current finance lease liabilities reported separately.
Constellium's net financing transaction exposure was zero for the period, with derivatives on financing transactions contributing a $32 million effect.
The company's total assets and total liabilities are tracked through consolidated balance sheets for 2024 and 2025.
Constellium's commitments and contingencies are noted in note 20 of the financial statements.
The company's ordinary shares have a par value of € (amount redacted) and additional paid-in capital is tracked through equity changes.
Constellium's non-controlling interests are tracked through transactions with non-controlling interests and total equity attribution for 2023-2025.
The company's share repurchase activity reduced treasury shares and total equity, with specific dollar amounts redacted in the provided text.
Constellium's allocation of treasury shares to share-based compensation plan vested is tracked annually.
The company's other comprehensive income/loss includes net change in post-employment benefit obligations, cash flow hedges, and currency translation adjustments for 2023-2025.
Constellium's total comprehensive income attributable to equity holders and non-controlling interests is reported annually for 2023-2025.
The company's basic earnings per share attributable to equity holders of Constellium is calculated using weighted-average ordinary shares outstanding for 2023-2025.
Constellium's diluted earnings per share includes the dilutive effect of non-vested restricted stock units and performance-based restricted stock units for 2023-2025.
